title: 10 best herbal tinctures for sore eyes
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for sore eyes are concentrated liquid extracts made from plants, which can provide natural relief from dry, irritated, or inflamed eyes. These tinctures work by soothing and calming the eyes, reducing redness and discomfort, and promoting healthy tear production. Examples of herbal tinctures that help with sore eyes include calendula, chamomile, and eyebright, which can be dropped directly onto the affected eye to provide instant relief. This can significantly improve daily life, allowing people to enjoy their usual activities without the discomfort and distraction caused by sore eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with sore eyes because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The aloe vera plant contains a number of beneficial compounds, including vitamins, minerals, and amino acids, that can help to reduce redness and irritation in the eyes. When used as an herbal tincture, these compounds are able to penetrate deeply into the affected area, providing fast and effective relief from sore eyes caused by dryness, fatigue, or minor injuries.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce redness, swelling, and pain in sore eyes.
Antioxidant properties: Aloe vera's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the delicate tissues of the eyes, promoting healing and reducing irritation.

Soothing and protective mucilage: Aloe vera's thick, gel-like substance (mucilage) coats and soothes the surface of the eye, protecting it from further irritation and discomfort while promoting the natural healing process.
remedy_parts: Roots: Aloe roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated eyes.
Leaves: The gel from aloe leaves contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as other nutrients that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves from the plant weighing approximately one pound or more.
Clean the harvested leaves thoroughly by rinsing them under cool running water and pat drying.
Slice the cleaned leaves into thin pieces and soak them in a high-proof vodka at a ratio of 1 part aloe to 3 parts vodka for 14 days.
After 14 days, strain the liquid mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and store it in a cool dark place.
Allow the tincture to mature for an additional 6 weeks before using it as needed by gently massaging a few drops onto affected eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower tinctures helps with sore eyes because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated eyes. The herb, also known as Echinacea purpurea, has been used for centuries to treat various eye issues, including conjunctivitis and dry eye syndrome. When applied topically or taken orally, the tincture's antioxidants and flavonoids work together to reduce redness, itchiness, and swelling, providing quick relief from sore eyes and promoting overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Purple Coneflower's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, alleviating discomfort and promoting healing.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sore eyes.

Immune-boosting properties: Purple Coneflower's immunomodulatory compounds stimulate the immune system to fight off infections that may be contributing to eye irritation or inflammation, promoting overall ocular health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds like triterpenoids, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with sore eyes.
Barks: Contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in soothing and calming irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers at the peak of blooming for optimal potency.
Chop the echinacea flowers into small pieces to increase surface area and aid extraction.
Combine 100g of chopped echinacea flowers with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring full coverage.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate infusion and maceration.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids, and store in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with sore eyes because it contains flavonoids, antioxidants, and carotenoids that work synergistically to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated tissues. The anti-inflammatory properties of pot marigold help to calm redness and itching, while its antioxidant compounds protect the delicate eye tissues from damage caused by free radicals. As a result, pot marigold tinctures provide natural relief for sore eyes, making it an effective remedy for dry eyes, conjunctivitis, and other eye irritations.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as calendic acid and apigenin, help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from itching, burning, and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold tincture, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, protect the eyes from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry eye syndrome, cataracts, and age-related macular degeneration.

Wound-healing properties: Pot marigold's triterpenoid saponins have been shown to promote wound healing and tissue repair, making it useful for treating conjunctivitis, corneal ulcers, and other eye irritations that can lead to scarring or adhesions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that soothe and calm irritated eye tissue.
Buds: Contain potent anti-inflammatory agents that reduce redness and swelling in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers in the morning after dew has dried for optimal potency.
Dry the calendula flowers using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours or air dry them for 24 hours.
Combine one part dried calendula flowers with two parts vodka or everclear in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking daily to facilitate extraction and maceration.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding solids after pressing on the filter.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tinctures helps with sore eyes because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that effectively soothe and calm irritated eyes. The herb's natural compounds, such as anthraglycosides and flavonoids, work to reduce redness, itchiness, and discomfort associated with dry eye syndrome, blepharitis, and conjunctivitis. By applying a few drops of eyebright tincture to the affected area, it helps to clear mucus and debris, promoting healthy tear flow and restoring visual clarity. As a result, eyebright tinctures provide fast and effective relief for sore eyes, allowing individuals to return to their normal activities with ease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Eyebright tincture contains flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from redness, itching, and irritation.
Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in eyebright tincture help neutralize free radicals that can damage eye tissues and contribute to soreness, fatigue, and dryness.

Anti-infective property: Eyebright's antiseptic properties help combat bacterial, viral, or fungal infections that can cause sore eyes, reducing the risk of complications and promoting faster healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Eyebright roots are used to make tinctures as they contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm irritated eyes.

Flowers: Eyebright flowers are used to make tinctures due to their rich content of anthocyanins and other pigments, which possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, making them effective in reducing redness and inflammation in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh euphrasia officinalis leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated, ensuring a high concentration of essential oils.
Wash the harvested leaves with filtered water to remove any dirt or debris, and gently pat dry with a clean cloth.
Combine 250 grams of dried euphrasia officinalis leaves with 750 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a glass jar, sealing tightly for 14 days.
Shake the jar twice daily for 2 weeks, allowing the mixture to steep and extract the herb's medicinal properties.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another glass container, discarding the solids and storing the resulting liquid in a dark bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with sore eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated tissues. The herb's flavonoids, apigenin, and luteolin, work together to reduce redness and swelling, alleviating discomfort and itchiness. As a natural antihistamine, chamomile also relieves itchy, watery eyes caused by allergies or dryness. Additionally, its gentle, non-irritating nature makes it an excellent choice for sensitive eyes, providing quick and effective relief from soreness and irritation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile tincture helps to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from redness and discomfort.

Antioxidant: Chamomile's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the delicate tissues of the eyes, reducing the risk of long-term eye damage.
Anxiolytic: The calming effects of chamomile tincture can help to reduce stress and anxiety-related eye strain, which is a common contributor to sore and tired eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used to make tinctures for sore eyes due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling.
Flowers: The delicate flowers are rich in apigenin, an antioxidant that soothes and calms irritated eyes, providing relief from itchiness and dryness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers in the morning when they are fully open.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator or air drying method at room temperature for 7-10 days.
Combine 100g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with 500ml of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 6 weeks, shaking it daily to extract the medicinal properties.
Strain and filter the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with sore eyes because of its calming and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal remedy contains linalool, a natural compound that soothes redness and irritation in the eyes. When used topically, it reduces swelling and relieves discomfort associated with dry eyes, conjunctivitis, or other eye irritations. Additionally, lavender's gentle sedative effect can help to relax tense eye muscles, promoting better eye health and reducing the risk of further strain. As a result, English lavender tinctures provide fast and effective relief for sore, itchy, or tired eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness and swelling associated with sore eyes, promoting comfort and relaxation.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of lavender tincture help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and promoting overall eye health.

Calming and sedative properties: Lavender's calming and sedative compounds can help alleviate eye strain, fatigue, and irritability, promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort associated with sore eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and reduce redness in sore eyes.

Buds: The buds are used as they contain a high concentration of essential oils, particularly linalool, which has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can benefit eye health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Combine the harvested flowers with 500ml of vodka in a clean glass jar at a ratio of 1:5.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Label and store the finished tincture at room temperature for up to 2 years, using 10-15 drops as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with sore eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The tannins present in the tree's leaves have been shown to reduce swelling and ease pain, providing fast relief from eye irritations. Additionally, the antioxidants in maidenhair tree help to protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthy and comfortable vision experience. Its soothing properties also aid in reducing redness and itching, making it an effective natural remedy for sore eyes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, alleviating soreness and discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory: The tannins and flavonoids present in Maidenhair Tree tincture have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with sore eyes.
Vasodilatory: Ginkgo biloba's ability to dilate blood vessels can increase blood flow to the eyes, promoting healing, reducing fatigue, and alleviating eye strain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, roots are used to reduce redness and swelling in sore eyes.
Bark: Containing soothing flavonoids, bark is used to calm irritated eye tissues and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh or dried ginkgo biloba leaves and chop them into small pieces for easy infusion.
Combine the chopped ginkgo biloba leaves with 1000ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring the herb is completely submerged.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it daily to facilitate extraction and maceration.
Strain the tincture mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, squeezing the cloth to extract as much liquid as possible.
Transfer the resulting ginkgo biloba tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, labeling them with the date and contents for future reference.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with sore eyes because they contain bioactive compounds that possess anti-inflammatory properties. The tannins and flavonoids present in dandelion extracts have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of conjunctivitis, blepharitis, and dry eye syndrome. By soothing the eyes and reducing redness, dandelion tinctures provide relief from irritation, discomfort, and visual disturbances associated with sore eyes. Additionally, their antioxidant properties help protect the delicate tissues of the eye from further damage, promoting a faster recovery process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce swelling and discomfort in the eyes, alleviating pain and redness.

Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage eye tissues, promoting healthy vision and protecting against age-related macular degeneration and other eye disorders.

Anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties: Dandelion's bioactive compounds have been shown to modulate the immune response, reducing inflammation in the eyes and preventing chronic conditions such as conjunctivitis and blepharitis.

Seeds: Containing omega-6 fatty acids and other beneficial nutrients, dandelion seeds have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and promote eye health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in a clean container.
Chop the plant material into small pieces and measure out 200g for every 500ml of vodka.
Combine the chopped plant material with the vodka in a clean glass jar and seal tightly.
Store the mixture in a dark place at room temperature for 6-8 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lamb's ear tinctures helps with sore eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herb, also known as Stellaria media, has been used for centuries to calm irritated eyes and reduce redness. When taken as a tincture, it absorbs quickly into the bloodstream, providing rapid relief from dryness, itchiness, and burning sensations associated with sore eyes. The tincture's antiseptic properties also help to prevent infection, promoting healing and reducing discomfort. As a result, lamb's ear tinctures have become a natural remedy for soothing and calming sore eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Lamb's ear contains anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid, which help reduce inflammation and redness in the eyes, alleviating symptoms of sore eyes such as swelling and itchiness.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in lamb's ear tincture, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye fatigue and discomfort.

Antibacterial and antiviral activities: Lamb's ear has been shown to exhibit antibacterial and antiviral properties, which can help combat infections that may be causing sore eyes, such as conjunctivitis or pink eye.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lamb's ear plant contain a high concentration of glycosides, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Leaves: Lamb's ear leaves have been traditionally used to make eye washes and tinctures due to their ability to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh stachys byzantina leaves in the morning after dew has dried for optimal potency.
Clean and chop one ounce of stachys byzantina leaves into small pieces to release essential oils.
Combine chopped stachys byzantina leaves with 100 proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar at a ratio of one part herb to three parts liquid.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking daily, to allow for maceration and extraction of active compounds.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding solids, and label with preparation date and dosing instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena tinctures helps with sore eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's flavonoids and phenolic compounds work to reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with dry or irritated eyes. By soothing the mucous membranes and calming the conjunctiva, lemon verbena tincture provides relief from itchiness and burning sensations, promoting a sense of calm and clarity in the affected area. Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds in lemon verbena tincture help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye fatigue, dryness, and irritation.

Calming and soothing properties: Lemon verbena has a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to reduce tension and stress that can exacerbate sore eyes, while its soothing properties can help to ease discomfort and promote relaxation.

Leaves: Contain citral, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that calms redness and itchiness in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Weigh and combine 10 grams of dried or 20 grams of fresh material with 100 milliliters of vodka.
Transfer the mixture to a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid and label it clearly.
Store the tincture in a dark cupboard for 14 days, shaking the jar twice daily.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with sore eyes because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated eyes. The herbal extract works to reduce redness, itchiness, and burning sensations caused by dry eyes or allergies. Additionally, valerian's sedative properties can help relax the muscles around the eye, reducing tension and pressure. Sedative: Valerian root has a sedative effect that can help calm the nervous system, reducing stress and promoting relaxation, which can alleviate eye strain and tension.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are rich in essential oils and sesquiterpenes, which make them an effective remedy for soothing inflammation and pain associated with sore eyes.
Buds: Valerian buds contain a high concentration of flavonoids and alkaloids that help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, making them a popular choice for treating irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried valeriana officinalis roots and cut them into small pieces suitable for extraction.
Combine the root pieces with 60% vodka in a glass jar at a ratio of 1 part herb to 5 parts solvent.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks to allow for maceration and extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar discarding the solids and preserving the liquid.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place for up to 6 months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tinctures helps with sore eyes because of its unique ability to soothe and calm irritated tissues. The herb's natural anti-inflammatory properties work to reduce redness and swelling, while its antioxidant-rich compounds help to protect the delicate skin around the eyes from further damage. Antioxidant properties: Comfrey's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can damage eye tissues and contribute to age-related macular degeneration, promoting overall eye health.

Soothing and protective mucilage properties: Comfrey tincture's mucilages (gum-like substances) form a protective film on the surface of the eyes, shielding them from further irritation and soothing dryness or itchiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of comfrey are rich in allantoin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Buds: Comfrey buds contain rosmarinic acid, an antioxidant that reduces inflammation and promotes healing, making them effective for treating sore eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh symphytum officinale leaves and stems in the morning when they are at their highest concentration of medicinal compounds.
Cut the plant material into small pieces and combine it with a neutral-tasting liquid such as vodka or ethanol at a ratio of 1 part herb to 3 parts solvent.
Place the mixture in a clean glass jar and seal it tightly, allowing it to steep for 14 days in a cool dark place.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container, squeezing the solids gently to extract as much liquid as possible.
Transfer the tincture to a dropper bottle and store it in the refrigerator at a temperature of 40°f (4°c) for up to one year, shaking well before each use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tinctures helps with sore eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated tissues. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried plantago major leaves measure one cup.
Chop the harvested plantago major leaves into small pieces using scissors.
Combine chopped plantago major leaves with 80 proof vodka measuring two cups.
Allow the mixture to steep for four weeks in a cool dark place shaking daily.
Strain the liquid and discard solids filtering through cheesecloth and store.

Combine one part of the dried leaves with 10 parts of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tinctures helps with sore eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive compounds in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with dry or irritated eyes. When applied topically, these compounds create a soothing effect that calms redness and itchiness, promoting healthy vision and reducing the risk of further irritation. This natural remedy can provide quick relief for sore eyes without harsh chemicals or artificial ingredients.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Thyme contains compounds like thymol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes.

Antibacterial and antifungal activity: Thyme's essential oils, such as carvacrol and eugenol, exhibit antimicrobial properties that can combat eye infections, reducing the risk of complications like conjunctivitis or corneal ulcers.

Antioxidant properties: Thyme tinctures contain antioxidants like flavonoids, which help neutralize free radicals in the eyes, protecting them from oxidative stress and potential damage caused by environmental toxins.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and discomfort in the eyes, making them an effective ingredient for soothing sore eyes.
Buds: Thyme buds contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and reduce inflammation, relieving soreness and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves or flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Rinse the plant material with distilled water and gently pat it dry with clean paper towels.
Combine 200g of the dried thymus vulgaris material with 500ml of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 14 days in a cool dark place shaking the jar twice daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean dark glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with sore eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herb contains hypericin, a bioflavonoid that has been shown to reduce inflammation and irritation in the eye tissues. When used as a tincture, St John's Wort can help to soothe red, itchy, and irritated eyes, providing fast relief from symptoms of dry eye syndrome, conjunctivitis, and other eye irritations. Its natural anti-inflammatory properties also help to calm and comfort the eyes, promoting healthy vision and overall ocular health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St. John's Wort contains antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals in the body, which can reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, alleviating soreness and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in St. John's Wort tincture have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from soreness and irritation.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: St. John's Wort has been traditionally used to treat anxiety and depression, which can contribute to eye strain and discomfort; its anxiolytic effects can help relax the eyes and reduce overall stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St John's Wort contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing and calming sore eyes.

Flowers: The flowers of St John's Wort are rich in hyperforin, a bioactive compound that has been shown to have antihistaminic and anti-allergic effects, helping to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with sore eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers, ensuring 100g of plant material.
Cleanse the plant material by gently washing with distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for solvent extraction.
Combine chopped plant material with 500ml of 95% ethanol in a glass jar, allowing maceration for 2 weeks.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, transferring it to dark glass bottles for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram tinctures helps with sore eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's bioactive compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes. Additionally, wild marjoram's antioxidant properties help to soothe and calm irritated eye tissues, reducing discomfort and improving vision clarity. By using a high-quality tincture made from organic wild marjoram, individuals can experience quick and effective relief from sore and tired eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Wild marjoram tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from inflammation and discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: The high antioxidant content in wild marjoram tincture helps protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and promoting healthy vision.

Soothing and calming effects: The tincture's flavonoids, such as luteolin and apigenin, have a calming effect on the eyes, helping to reduce itchiness, irritation, and discomfort associated with sore eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help reduce redness and swelling associated with sore eyes.
Leaves: Containing soothing flavonoids, leaves provide gentle relief from itchy and irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried oregano leaves from a trusted source and measure out 20% of the total weight in grams.
Combine the measured oregano leaves with 80% vodka by weight in a clean glass jar ensuring thorough coverage.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking occasionally to facilitate extraction.
After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles, label and date them, and store in a cool dark place for up to 5 years.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat sore eyes with herbal tinctures is by using a combination of Echinacea and Goldenseal. Mix 10-15 drops of each tincture in a small bowl and add a few drops of distilled water. Soak a cotton ball in the solution and apply it to your closed eyes for 5-7 minutes. This will help reduce inflammation, ease discomfort, and promote healing. Repeat as needed up to three times daily.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to sore eyes that are treated with herbal tinctures are ear infections, sinusitis, and throat irritations. Tinctures made from herbs such as goldenseal, echinacea, and yarrow have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and heal these conditions. These herbs can be used topically or taken internally to alleviate symptoms like pain, swelling, and congestion, providing natural relief for a range of ear, nose, and throat issues.
